mission.title: Install and Configure Submariner on Kubernetes
mission.description: Submariner is a networking component designed to interconnect Pods and Services across multiple Kubernetes clusters, enabling seamless communication. Installing Submariner allows you to create secure and efficient networking between your clusters, regardless of the underlying network plugins.

steps.title: Add the Submariner Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Submariner Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add submariner https://submariner-io.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Submariner using Helm
steps.description:
Install Submariner with the specified version in a new namespace called 'submariner':
```bash
helm install submariner submariner/submariner --namespace submariner --create-namespace --version v0.22.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Submariner pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n submariner
```

steps.title: Configure Submariner for your clusters
steps.description:
Run the Submariner configuration command to set up the necessary connections between your clusters:
```bash
subctl deploy-broker --kubeconfig=<path-to-your-kubeconfig>
```

steps.title: Validate Submariner is working
steps.description:
Validate that Submariner is functioning properly by checking the status:
```bash
subctl validate --kubeconfig=<path-to-your-kubeconfig>
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Submariner will show all pods in the 'submariner' namespace as running. Additionally, the validation command should confirm that the setup is correct and functioning as expected.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add submariner https://submariner-io.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install submariner submariner/submariner --namespace submariner --create-namespace --version v0.22.1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes version 1.24 or higher, and have both Helm and kubectl installed and configured.
